newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. The controlling story is unchanged: the pending Skyworks stock-and-cash acquisition (China SAMR review in Phase II, close guided early 2027, hopeful late 2026) makes QRVO trade as a merger-arb proxy on SWKS, capping standalone upside. Today's -3.24% appears flow-driven, tracking RF/handset semi weakness rather than any new fundamental datapoint. Recent insider selling (CEO Bruggeworth $5.8M on June 1 under a 10b5-1 plan; CEO + Director Peter Feld >$198M cumulatively under disclosed plans) is mildly negative context but pre-dates or is incidental to the window.

sources.label: StockTitan - Skyworks/Qorvo 425: SAMR Phase II, late-2026 close hoped
FinancialContent/StockStory - Photronics, Qorvo, TI shares falling (sector flow)
Skyworks IR - Skyworks and Qorvo to combine ($22B RF/analog leader)

verdicts.rankRationale: Consistent with yesterday's 'remove' call: QRVO has weak direct AI-node bottleneck exposure (no HBM/advanced-packaging/CPO leverage), and the pending Skyworks acquisition removes standalone optionality, so it should keep sliding toward the bottom. Today's -3.2% is sector/arb flow, not new evidence, hence only a drift-sized delta.
verdicts.bull: Merger arb plus SWKS synergy realization ($500M+ targeted) gives a defined-return path; defense/5G RF and FQ4 beat (EPS $1.69 vs $1.21, GM 53%) show the asset is not broken.
verdicts.neutral: Deal-locked equity that tracks SWKS plus a spread; AI-node relevance is tangential, so neither AI capex upside nor downside changes the case much.
verdicts.bear: SAMR Phase II is a live China-veto risk on a US RF consolidation; if the deal breaks, QRVO reverts to a cyclical handset RF name with heavy insider selling above $100.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: SAMR Phase II decision / merger closing timeline updates
verdicts.primaryRisk: China regulatory block of the Skyworks deal collapsing the arb and re-exposing handset RF cyclicality

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO-led alpha list QRVO has essentially no thesis: zero co-packaged-optics relevance, and the Skyworks deal caps any hidden-optionality upside to deal terms. No new info since yesterday's 'remove' verdict, so this is continued drift down rather than a fresh call.
verdicts.bull: Defined merger consideration provides downside support, and combined SWKS/QRVO scale could fund non-handset diversification (defense, power) post-close.
verdicts.neutral: It is a merger-arb vehicle, not a CPO bottleneck play; the -3.2% day move mirrors SWKS/sector flow and does not change the alpha math.
verdicts.bear: Crowded arb with SAMR tail risk; the >$198M of disclosed insider selling near highs signals sellers do not see residual upside beyond deal terms.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Merger milestones (SAMR clearance, shareholder/regulatory steps) tightening the spread
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deal break on China review leaving a handset-cyclical RF name with no CPO story
